sections.paragraph:
ἀπομάσσω (μάσσω of hand motions used, e.g., in kneading or molding; Aristoph. et al.; POxy 1381, 133; Tob 7:16 S) wipe off, mid. oneself (Galen, Protr. 1, p. 26 [10 p. 34, 5 John]; Ps.-Callisth. 1, 18, 10) τὸν κονιορτὸν ἀπομασσόμεθα ὑμῖν we wipe off the dust (in protest) against you Lk 10:11. S. on ἐκτινάσσω.—DELG s.v. μάσσω.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

Aristoph Aristoph , V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Protr. Protr. = Protreptic i quae supersunt

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 118
